class.name: MultiNode
class.brief_description: Compositional instancing parent that manages a shared list of instance transforms and data.
class.description: MultiNode is the root of a compositional instancing system. It owns the master instance list — transforms, dirty flags, and per-instance data — shared by all child nodes. Add MultiNodeSub-derived children (Mesh, Collider, Audio, etc.) to define what each instance is and does. One MultiNode with sub-nodes replaces thousands of individual scene nodes.

signal.name: instances_changed
signal.description: Emitted when the instance list changes — count, transforms, or data. All child sub-nodes listen to this signal to stay in sync.

members.member: When enabled, the parent stores a Transform3D per instance. Disable to save memory when you only need data arrays and compute shaders without spatial positioning. Sub-nodes that require transforms (MultiNode3D and below) will not function when disabled.

The current number of instances. Set this directly to resize the instance list. Triggers a full sync to all child sub-nodes. Not exposed in the inspector — use add_instance() and remove_instance() from scripts.
Maximum number of instances allowed. Set to 0 for unlimited. add_instance() returns -1 when the limit is reached. If the current count exceeds a newly set limit, instances are trimmed.
Number of floats stored per instance in the parent-level data array. Use for shared game state accessible from all sub-nodes and GDScript. Minimum 1, maximum 64.

Number of floats per instance in each sub-node's private data array. Must be a multiple of 4 for GPU alignment (automatically rounded up). Controls the stride for all sub-node data arrays and compute shader binding 2.

method.name: add_instance
method.description: Add a new instance with the given transform. Returns the new instance index, or -1 if the instance_limit has been reached.

method.name: remove_instance
method.description: Remove the instance at the given index. Uses swap-and-pop internally — the last instance moves into the removed slot, so indices may change.

method.name: clear_instances
method.description: Remove all instances. Resets the count to zero and frees all internal buffers.

method.name: set_instance_transform
method.description: Set the transform for a single instance. Marks it dirty and notifies children at end of frame (or at end_batch if batching).

method.name: get_instance_transform
method.description: Get the current transform for an instance.

method.name: set_all_transforms
method.description: Replace all instance transforms at once. Resizes the instance count to match the array size.

method.name: set_positions
method.description: Convenience method that sets identity-basis transforms from an array of positions. Resizes the instance count to match.

method.name: get_transform_buffer
method.description: Returns all instance transforms as an array. Useful for serialization or bulk reads.

method.name: mark_dirty
method.description: Manually mark a single instance as dirty. Children will update it on next sync. Normally called automatically by set_instance_transform.

method.name: mark_all_dirty
method.description: Mark all instances as dirty. Forces a full re-sync of all children on the next signal.

method.name: is_dirty
method.description: Check whether an instance is marked dirty since the last sync.

method.name: get_dirty_count
method.description: Returns the number of currently dirty instances. Children use this to decide between sparse and full updates.

method.name: begin_batch
method.description: Enter batch mode. Transform changes are accumulated silently without emitting signals. Call end_batch() when done to emit a single instances_changed signal.

method.name: end_batch
method.description: Exit batch mode and emit a single instances_changed signal for all accumulated changes.

method.name: notify_transforms_changed
method.description: Manually trigger an instances_changed signal. Use when you modify transforms via direct buffer access from C++ and need children to update.

method.name: set_instance_data
method.description: Set a single float in the parent-level per-instance data array. Channel is the offset within the instance's data (0 to data_stride-1).

method.name: get_instance_data
method.description: Read a single float from the parent-level per-instance data array.

method.name: set_instance_data_vec
method.description: Set the entire data vector for a single instance. The array is clamped to data_stride length.

method.name: get_instance_data_vec
method.description: Get the full data vector for a single instance as a PackedFloat32Array.
